code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Defective/unsafe products or complete failure
    - Explicit return/disposal recommendations
    - Safety hazards override any positive remarks
    - Multiple failure incidents across purchases

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Functional deficiencies overshadow benefits
    - Regret language ("waste of money", "misleading")
    - Failed core functionality despite some positives

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons without clear dominance
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Brief positive statements without enthusiastic language
    - Single functional criticism with/without explicit star deduction
    - Positive experience with 1-2 minor drawbacks
    - "Almost perfect" language without strong recommendation

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Enthusiastic endorsement ("love", "best ever", "exceeded")
    - Multiple complementary aspects praised (3+ features)
    - Long-term validation of performance (6+ months)
    - Practical benefits outweigh stated minor limitations
    - Strong recommendation despite non-functional quirks

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - "Perfect except [non-functional quirk]" →5
    - "Would be 5 stars but [functional issue]" →4
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of features
    - Safety issues immediately negate positives →1
    - Temporary issues resolved enthusiastically →5
    - Inherent material properties ≠ product flaws
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1 (5→4 Error Fix):
Review: "This trash can has silent operation, perfect size, and durable build. Minor lid alignment quirk."
<Reasoning>Multiple strengths with trivial non-functional drawback. Comprehensive satisfaction.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2 (4→5 Error Fix):
Review: "Not much to say. They work!"
<Reasoning>Brief positive lacks enthusiastic language or detailed endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3 (5-Star Benchmark):
Review: "Love everything! Silent, space-saving, durable. Best purchase this year!"
<Reasoning>Enthusiastic endorsement with multiple features praised.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4 (Borderline 4):
Review: "Excellent blender except slightly loud. Still recommend for performance."
<Reasoning>Single functional criticism with maintained recommendation.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5 (1-Star Critical):
Review: "Failed immediately - leaked dangerous chemicals. Fire hazard!"
<Reasoning>Safety-critical failure overrides potential positives.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 6 (5-Star Update):
Review: "UPDATE: After 1 year, still perfect! Minor noise irrelevant."
<Reasoning>Long-term validation outweighs trivial drawback.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7 (2-Star Regret):
Review: "Beautiful but cracks under normal use. Wouldn't recommend."
<Reasoning>Core failure despite aesthetic appeal.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8 (3-Star Compromise):
Review: "Gets job done with frequent adjustments. Okay for price."
<Reasoning>Adequate performance with usability compromises.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 9 (4-Star Imperfect):
Review: "Great value but lid fits loosely. Holds items securely."
<Reasoning>Minor functional issue balanced with positive aspects.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10 (5-Star Material):
Review: "Cotton wrinkles but absorbs better than competitors. Worth it!"
<Reasoning>Material property outweighed by core functionality.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>
</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (36 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 14
  gold 4 -> 5: 10
  gold 4 -> 3: 6
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 1 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Very nice set of skillets - and what a lovely color.  Quite a bargain, and I was glad to find it as I provide my husband his "own" skillets.  He tends to be a "high heat" cook, and so I don't let him use my expensive skillets.  Was nice to find Farbe..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 2, 1] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 36 errors, 18 borderline out of 80 examples."}
